subject: Generating an upsampled gain for super-resolution processing in M
body:
Hi all,
I'm trying to process data collected in Tomo5 using the Falcon4i and want 
to go to super res with Warp. My gain reference off the microscope is 4K, 
and I would like to upsample to 8K. I've tried using EMAN2's e2proc2d to do 
this: 

e2proc2d.py --clip=8192,8192 --scale 2 --outtype=mrc 4K_GainReference.gain 
8K_GainReference.mrc

However this is seemingly normalizing the pixel intensity in the resulting 
MRC and it totally washes out the gain artifacts. As is, the data will go 
past Nyquist even with the poor gain reference, however I would like to get 
this properly sorted. I wanted to reach out to the group to see if anyone 
has any tips or tricks for this?

Best,
Robert
